parameters.content:
## 1. Getting messages IN from Discord

Discord has no native outgoing webhook, and n8n's built-in Discord node is send-only. Two options to feed this Webhook node:

**A) Community trigger node (easiest):** install `n8n-nodes-discord-trigger` (Settings → Community Nodes), then replace the Webhook + Normalize nodes with it.

**B) Tiny bridge bot:** run a small Discord bot that POSTs `{ "content", "channelId", "author" }` to this webhook URL whenever a message mentions it.

The rest of the workflow works with either.

## 2. Credentials to attach after import

• **Discord Webhook** credential on both Discord nodes (create a webhook in your Discord channel: Channel Settings → Integrations → Webhooks)
• **OpenAI** credential on the chat model (or swap the node for Anthropic/Gemini/Ollama)
• **SerpAPI** credential on the web search tool (or swap for another search tool)

## 3. Video transcripts

The `get_video_transcript` tool points at a placeholder URL. Point it at a transcript service:
• YouTube: any transcript API (e.g. Supadata, youtube-transcript.io)
• Facebook: usually needs download + transcription (e.g. self-hosted yt-dlp + Whisper endpoint). Same tool, same `{video_url}` placeholder.

## 4. Obsidian

An Obsidian vault is just a folder. Edit the path in **Write to Obsidian Vault** to your vault's folder (n8n must run on the same machine, or mount the vault into the Docker container). Alternative: Obsidian's *Local REST API* plugin + an HTTP Request node.

options.systemMessage:
You are a research assistant that users reach via Discord. You receive a single instruction and must complete it using your tools, then produce a markdown note for the user's Obsidian vault.

Your tools:
- web_search: search the web for current information
- fetch_webpage: fetch the full content of a specific URL
- get_video_transcript: fetch the transcript of a video (YouTube or Facebook) given its URL. Use this before summarizing any video.

Process:
1. Understand the request. If it references a video URL, get its transcript first. If it needs current information, search the web, then fetch the most promising pages.
2. Do the work: summarize, research, or answer as instructed.
3. Produce your final answer as JSON with exactly these fields:
   - title: a short, filename-safe title for the note (no slashes, colons or quotes)
   - markdown: the complete Obsidian note. Start with YAML frontmatter (title, date, source URLs, tags), then a well-structured markdown summary with headings, key points, and citations/links to sources.
   - reply: a short, friendly Discord message (max ~1500 chars) summarizing what you did and the key takeaway. Use Discord-flavored markdown.

Always fill all three fields, even for simple questions (the note then records your findings).

parameters.toolDescription: Fetch the full HTML/text content of a specific webpage. Use after web_search to read a promising result in full.

values.name: url
values.description: The full URL of the webpage to fetch, including https://

parameters.toolDescription: Get the transcript of a video from YouTube or Facebook. Pass the full video URL. Returns the spoken content as text. Always use this before summarizing a video.

values.name: video_url
values.description: The full URL of the YouTube or Facebook video

parameters.jsonSchemaExample:
{
  "title": "How Solid State Batteries Work",
  "markdown": "---
title: How Solid State Batteries Work
date: 2026-07-18
source: https://example.com
tags: [summary, video]
---

# Summary

Key points here...",
  "reply": "Done! I summarized the video — key takeaway: ... Full note saved to your vault."
}

parameters.content:
={{ $('AI Agent').item.json.output.reply }}

📝 Saved to vault: `{{ $('Write to Obsidian Vault').item.json.fileName }}`
